codex_response:
Implemented the ARC2 package for `bae5c565`. The rule is: erase the top row, keep the vertical `8` spine, and rebuild the top-row sequence as a downward-growing centered wedge, with the spine replacing the centered background `5`.

The summary hint was usable and consistent with the official examples, so I did not add a `notes.md`. No new primitive was needed, and I did not touch `arc2/dsl_ext.py`.

- [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_bae5c565/__init__.py#L1)

- [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_bae5c565/generator.py#L4)

The generator stays close to the task family: odd square grids, gray background, centered `8` spine starting near the middle, a top row with exactly one centered `5`, and bottom settling with 0-1 fully settled rows, which matches the training examples’ silhouette and layout priors. I also visually checked [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/bae5c565/previews/preview_01.png) and it looks on-distribution.
